task_completion.description: Navigate to the CAMEO Chemicals website.

task_completion.description: Correctly classify Sodium and identify its hazardous gas in the report.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The report lists Sodium as Water-Reactive: YES and mentions the production of hydrogen gas.

task_completion.description: Correctly classify Calcium Carbide and identify its hazardous gas in the report.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The report lists Calcium Carbide as Water-Reactive: YES and mentions the production of acetylene gas.

task_completion.description: Correctly classify Acetone in the report.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The report lists Acetone as Water-Reactive: NO.

task_completion.description: Correctly classify Potassium and identify its hazardous gas in the report.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The report lists Potassium as Water-Reactive: YES and mentions the production of hydrogen gas.

task_completion.description: Correctly classify Ethanol in the report.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The report lists Ethanol as Water-Reactive: NO.

task_completion.description: Save the report to the specified file path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal shows the file saved exactly at /home/ga/Documents/water_reactivity_report.txt.

integrity.description: The agent actually used a web browser to search for the chemicals on CAMEO Chemicals.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent typing chemical names or CAS numbers into the CAMEO Chemicals search bar and viewing the results, rather than just hallucinating the answers.

integrity.description: The agent created the report file in the actual system environment.
integrity.visual_evidence: The file is seen open in a text editor (like gedit, nano, or vim) or verified via a terminal command like 'cat' or 'ls' at the correct path.

integrity.description: The agent did not use a script with hardcoded answers.
integrity.visual_evidence: There is no evidence of a python or bash script containing the exact answers (YES/NO and gas names) being executed to bypass the web search.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Sodium (CAS 7440-23-5): Water-Reactive: YES, produces hydrogen gas. Calcium Carbide (CAS 75-20-7): Water-Reactive: YES, produces acetylene gas. Acetone (CAS 67-64-1): Water-Reactive: NO. Potassium (CAS 7440-09-7): Water-Reactive: YES, produces hydrogen gas. Ethanol (CAS 64-17-5): Water-Reactive: NO.
